SEQUENCES

Primers for sequencing cDNAs in pJG4-5 from the 5' end can be derived
from the sequence of the fusion moiety shown below ("5' sequence").

For sequencing from the 3' end, primers can be derived from sequences
in the ADH1 (ADC1) terminator; the portion in pJG4-5 corresponds to
sequences downstream of the natural HindIII site in the ADH1
terminator.  The following sequence ("3' sequence") is from this
terminator region, about 40 bp downstream of the HindIII site.  We
have used primers complementary to this region for sequencing and PCR.

pJG4-5 3' sequence

cDNA..XhoI..HindIII...ADHterm...5'...GTCTCCAATCAAGGTTGTCGGCTTGTCTACCTTGCCAGA
AATTTACGAAAAGATGGAAAAGGGTC.....3'
   

pJG4-5 5' sequence

Fusion moiety from pJG4-5
(Linear) MAP of: N-Terminus.Txt  check: 5219  from: 1  to: 345

Acid activator.Txt  Length: 345  June 14, 1991  13:26
Sequence of HinDIII-HinDIII fragment encoding the Sv40 nuclear
localization sequence, the B42 activation region, and the HA1
epitope from the influenza virus hemagglutinin gene, and containing
the EcoR1 and XhoI cloning sites.

 With 177 enzymes: *

                             September 18, 1991  19:30  ..

pEG202 Sequence

Sequence of pEG202, the standard bait expression plasmid.  This seeuqence
includes part of the ADH promoter, the LexA coding sequence, the polylinker
3' to LexA (begining around position 1370 with the EcoRI site), and part of
the ADH terminator)

REFORMAT of: Eg202p.Seq  check: -1  from: 1  to: 1882  July 13, 1992  15:20

 (No documentation)

Eg202p.Seq  Length: 1743  November 25, 1992  14:20  Type: N  Check: 5483  ..

       1  CGATTTTTTT CTAAACCGTG GAATATTTCG GATATCCTTT TGTTGTTTCC
